category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

surface.name: Arduino Cloud Application API via API key (hosted)
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation presents this as the hosted path from account setup to Authenticate to the Arduino Cloud Application API. It reaches the requested first-success boundary without extending into post-success production work.

entry_point.developer_intent: Arduino Cloud accepts the application credentials and returns an access token for hosted API use.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no prior Arduino setup for this journey.
The journey starts at account creation and stops at the first documented successful outcome.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Authenticate to Arduino Cloud Application API
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Arduino Cloud accepts the application credentials and returns an access token for hosted API use.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The authentication request returns a bearer access token without an authentication error.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official documentation leads the developer to: Authenticate to the Arduino Cloud Application API.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first observable happy-path result on the selected Arduino Cloud Application API via API key (hosted) surface; later configuration and production hardening occur after success.

prerequisites.requirement: An Arduino account with access to Arduino Cloud.
An Arduino Cloud API key and client secret.
An HTTPS client such as cURL.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to an Arduino account and open Arduino Cloud.
primary_path.input: A new developer starting at account creation
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Create an API key in Arduino Cloud and copy its client ID and client secret.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Submit the documented client-credentials authentication request to Arduino Cloud.
primary_path.output: The authentication endpoint returns an access token.
primary_path.success_signal: The authentication endpoint returns an access token.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Confirm that the response contains a bearer access token usable with the Application API.
primary_path.output: A non-empty access token is present.
primary_path.success_signal: A non-empty access token is present.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

friction_gates.description: The client secret is shown as a sensitive credential and must be retained securely.

sources.title: Authenticate to the Arduino Cloud Application API
sections_used: Authenticate to the Arduino Cloud Application API
evidence_supported: Arduino Cloud accepts the application credentials and returns an access token for hosted API use.
The authentication request returns a bearer access token without an authentication error.

uncertainties.question: Does the current fresh-account UI exactly match every navigation label in the documented Authenticate to the Arduino Cloud Application API path?
uncertainties.impact: Navigation wording may differ while the required outcome and boundary remain the same.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation establishes the journey but account-specific UI state can vary.
